root_text.sn24.18:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.18:1.3: ‘neva hoti, na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.18:2.1: “Rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.18:2.2: ‘neva hoti, na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti …pe….
root_text.sn24.18:3.4: vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.18:3.5: ‘neva hoti, na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.18:3.7: “Yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn24.18:3.9: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vā taṁ sukhaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn24.18:3.11: “Yaṁ panāniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhammaṁ, api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.18:3.12: ‘neva hoti, na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.18:4.1: “Yato kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa imesu ca ṭhānesu kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti, dukkhepissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti, dukkhasamudayepissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti, dukkhanirodhepissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti, dukkhanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāyapissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti—
root_text.sn24.18:4.2: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako sotāpanno avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyano”ti.
root_text.sn24.18:5.2: so attā no ca me siyā;
root_text.sn24.18:6.3: Anantavā ca taṁ jīvaṁ taṁ sarīranti,
root_text.sn24.18:7.3: Neva hoti na na hoti tathāgato paraṁ maraṇāti.

translation_text.sn24.18:0.3: A Realized One Neither Still Exists Nor No Longer Exists
translation_text.sn24.18:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, does the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.18:1.3: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’?”
translation_text.sn24.18:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn24.18:2.1: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.18:2.2: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’ …
translation_text.sn24.18:3.4: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.18:3.5: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’?”
translation_text.sn24.18:3.7: “That which is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, and explored by the mind: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn24.18:3.9: “But if it’s impermanent, is it suffering or happiness?”
translation_text.sn24.18:3.11: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.18:3.12: ‘A realized one neither still exists nor no longer exists after death’?”
translation_text.sn24.18:4.1: “When a noble disciple has given up doubt regarding these six grounds, and has given up doubt in suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation,
translation_text.sn24.18:4.2: they’re called a noble disciple who is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
